steps.title: Identify the unsuitable-coin crisis
steps.content:
The passage identifies that coins below 80% silver are unsuitable for trade, and Sidon's 367 BCE coins fell below this threshold, triggering a confidence crisis.

To relieve this crisis, Abd'aštart I (Ba'alšillem II's successor) would need to make coins trade-suitable again—that is, restore them above 80% silver.

steps.title: Apply the suitability criterion to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B keeps the absolute amount of silver per coin constant (preserving intrinsic value) while reducing the total coin weight, which automatically raises the silver percentage above the 80% threshold and restores trade-suitability.

The other choices either maintain unsuitable coins, arbitrarily raise standards without improving coins, or address mining rather than minting.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
